title: anchusa officinalis subsp. intacta
intro: Anchusa officinalis subsp. intacta, also known as intact alkanet, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, known for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The plant is rich in alkanin, a pigment that is used as a natural food coloring, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions. Anchusa officinalis subsp. intacta is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and it is also used as a natural dye in cosmetics and textiles.
